(SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE DETAILS) THE COURT WILL SIGN COLE ASHBURY'S PROPOSED ASSIGNMENT ORDER UNLESS IT RECEIVES OBJECTIONS BY NOVEMBER 12, 2025. PROPOSED ORDER SUBMITTED. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Permanently Seal Documents
Off Calendar Nov-21-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

RESPONDENT COOKIES CREATIVE CONSULTING & PROMOTIONS, INC.'S Motion To Permanently Seal Documents.

The unopposed motion to seal is granted.

This motion to conceal concerns Exhibits 1 through 12 (Volumes 1-3) attached to the Supplemental Declaration of Parker Berling in Support of Opposition to Motion for Assignment Order, and Exhibits 1 through 5, Exhibits 7 through 10, and Exhibit 12 attached to the Filed Under Seal Declaration of Bart Dalton in Support of Sur-Reply Brief in Support of Motion for Assignment Order. The court finds that these materials contain confidential trade secrets, including financial information and licensee lists. As to these materials, the court finds that (1) there exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the record; (2) the overriding interest supports sealing the record; (3) a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; (4) the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and (5) no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling. Proposed order is to identify the exact date of filing of the document to be sealed, complete title of the document to be sealed and E-filing transcation number of the filed document.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Sets No. 1 And 2, And To Interrogatories, Set No. 1; Request For Order Awarding Monetary Sanctions Against Judgment Debtor In The Sum Of $5,100
Off Calendar Nov-12-2025 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Assignment Order; Memorandum; Declaration Of Bart Dalton
Off Calendar Nov-07-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

The unopposed motion for an assignment order is granted and the judgment debtor Cookies Creative Consulting & Promotions, Inc. is ordered to assign its rights to payments by the entities listed in Appendix 1 to the Delaplane Declaration to the judgment creditor Cole Ashbury Group LLC.

Code of Civil Procedure, sec. 708.510, states in relevant part:

"(a) Except as otherwise provided by law, upon application of the judgment creditor on noticed motion, the court may order the judgment debtor to assign to the judgment creditor . . . all or part of a right to payment due or to become due, whether or not the right is conditioned on future developments . . . .
"(b) The notice of the motion shall be served on the judgment debtor. Service shall be made personally or by mail.
"(c) Subject to subdivisions (d), (e), and (f), in determining whether to order an assignment or the amount of an assignment pursuant to subdivision (a), the court may take into consideration all relevant factors, including the following:
"(1) The reasonable requirements of a judgment debtor who is a natural person and of persons supported in whole or in part by the judgment debtor.
"(2) Payments the judgment debtor is required to make or that are deducted in satisfaction of other judgments and wage assignments, including earnings assignment orders for support.
"(3) The amount remaining due on the money judgment.
"(4) The amount being or to be received in satisfaction of the right to payment that may be assigned.
"(d) A right to payment may be assigned pursuant to this article only to the extent necessary to satisfy the money judgment."

Cole Ashbury's request for a blanket assignment order as to all third parties is denied. The Judgment Creditor shall file a new motion for each third party it discovers and seeks an assignment order for.

Cole Ashbury delivered a physical courtesy copy of the proposed assignment order but failed to attach it to their electronic filings. Cole Ashbury is advised to remember to file an electronic version of all parts of their submitted documents.

The court will sign Cole Ashbury's proposed assignment order unless it receives objections by November 12, 2025.

Proposed order submitted.

Visiting Judge: Dennis Hayashi; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/DH)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Turnover Order; Declaration In Support; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities ***Redacted***
Off Calendar Nov-07-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER COLE ASHBURY GROUP, LLC'S Motion For Turnover Order

Plaintiff Cole Ashbury Group, LLC's motion for turnover order for the possession of property and documentary evidence of title to "The Cookies Bus," a tour bus, to Cole Ashbury Group as a judgment creditor is granted.

Defendant Cookies Creative Consulting & Promotions, Inc. is restrained from the sale, alienation, mortgage, lien, encumbrance, or exploitation of the tour bus. Cookies is ordered to turn over documents of title, instruments, securities, money, and any other documents or papers demonstrating ownership of the tour bus to the Sheriff of the City and County of San Francisco as identified in the moving papers no later than January 19, 2026, to be sold at a Sheriff's auction.

Defendant's sole grounds for opposing this motion is to request Plaintiff remove any branding from the bus at Plaintiff's own expense. This request is denied.

Proposed order submitted.

Visiting Judge: Dennis Hayashi; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/DH)

Notice Of Motion For Assignment Order; Declaration; Memorandum
Off Calendar Sep-25-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PETITIONER COLE ASHBURY GROUP, LLC's Motion For Assignment Order.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The motion for an assignment order is granted and the judgment debtor Cookies Creative Consulting & Promotions, Inc. is ordered to assign its rights to payments by the entities listed in Appendix 1 to the Delaplane Declaration, submitted with the original moving papers, to the judgment creditor Cole Ashbury Group LLC (see word minutes for complete ruling.)

Order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

Defendant Cookies Creative Consulting & Promotions, Inc. is to submit for filing additional evidence along with a supplemental declaration by September 11, 2025. Plaintiff Cole Ashbury Group, LLC is to submit for filing a surreply by September 18, 2025. The matter is hereby continued for further hearing to September 25, 2025, at 9:00 A.M. in Department 301.
